title: carex × innocua
intro: Carex × innocua, also known as Wood Sedge, is a hybrid plant species within the Carex genus, typically found in wetland environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Wood Sedge include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Carex × innocua can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
